ogTitle: Jackson South vs Jacobs Well: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Jackson South and Jacobs Well.
heading: Jackson South vs Jacobs Well — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Jackson South, Queensland and Jacobs Well, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Jackson South has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to Jacobs Well (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Jackson South or Jacobs Well safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Jackson South has a flood risk score of 25/100, while Jacobs Well scores 49/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Jackson South scores 34/100 for bushfire risk. Jacobs Well scores 36/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Jackson South or Jacobs Well?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Jackson South has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.05% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
